---
title: ""
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.hero-block}
# Portfolio Construction and Risk Analysis

::: {.subtitle}
A rigorous treatment of quantitative methods for investment management
:::

::: {.authors}
R. Douglas Martin &nbsp;·&nbsp; Thomas K. Philips &nbsp;·&nbsp; Bernd Scherer &nbsp;·&nbsp; Kirk Li
:::

::: {.publisher-badge}
Springer &nbsp;|&nbsp; Planned Publication: 2026
:::
:::

::: {.copyright-notice}
**Copyright Notice.** Material on this site is planned for publication in 2026 by R. Douglas Martin, Thomas K. Philips, Bernd Scherer, and Kirk Li. All rights reserved. © Copyright 2025.
:::

## About the Book

*Portfolio Construction and Risk Analysis* (PCRA) provides a comprehensive and rigorous account of the quantitative foundations of modern portfolio management. The book develops the mathematical, statistical, and computational tools needed to construct, evaluate, and risk-manage investment portfolios in a principled way.

The text integrates classical theory — mean-variance optimization, the Capital Asset Pricing Model, the Arbitrage Pricing Theory — with modern statistical methodology including robust estimation, regularized regression, and machine learning. Throughout, the authors emphasize practical implementation alongside theoretical justification.

## Preface

The [Preface](preface.qmd) provides an overview of the book's 16 chapters and 5 appendices, describes the reproducibility theme and supporting R packages (`PCRA`, `facmodCS`, `facmodTS`, `robustGarch`), motivates the use of robust estimators, introduces the special focus topics in Chapters 10–16, and includes data use disclaimers for CRSP®, SPGMI, ICE, Tradeweb, S&P, and CBOE datasets.

## Vignettes

The book is accompanied by three vignettes that provide hands-on documentation for the `PCRA` R package and its data. Full source and PDFs are available on [GitHub](https://github.com/robustport/PCRA/tree/main/vignettes).

::: {.appendix-grid}

::: {.appendix-card}
::: {.label}
Vignette 1
:::
### PCRA Package and Data Overview
Structure and contents of the `PCRA` package: the 31 included data sets, the two package versions (CRAN and development), `data.table` basics, the CRSP® and SPGMI datasets, and the non-open-source data policy.

[Read Vignette 1](vignette-1.qmd){.btn-appendix}
:::

::: {.appendix-card}
::: {.label}
Vignette 2
:::
### CRSP Stocks and SPGMI Factors
Deep dive into `stocksCRSPmonthly` and `factorsSPGMIr`: data structure, the `selectCRSPandSPGMI` function, `xts` time series manipulation, sector/capitalization subsetting, weekly and daily CRSP data.

[Read Vignette 2](vignette-2.qmd){.btn-appendix}
:::

::: {.appendix-card}
::: {.label}
Vignette 3
:::
### PCRA Reproducibility
How to access and run the chapter demo R scripts: the `demo()` command, copy/paste workflow, sourcing with `rstudioapi`, character of the scripts, and setup of `devtools` and Rtools.

[Read Vignette 3](vignette-3.qmd){.btn-appendix}
:::

:::

## Demo Code

Chapter-by-chapter R scripts that reproduce the figures and tables in the book are hosted in the `demo` folder of the PCRA GitHub repository. The [Demo Code](demo-code.qmd) page lists all available scripts with direct GitHub links and instructions for running them.

[View Demo Code](demo-code.qmd){.btn-appendix}

## Online Appendices

This site hosts the five technical appendices to PCRA. Each appendix provides self-contained background material that underpins the methods and results developed in the main text. Full PDF downloads are available on each appendix page.

::: {.appendix-grid}

::: {.appendix-card}
::: {.label}
Appendix A
:::
### Mathematical Tools for PCRA
Vectors and vector spaces, matrix algebra, eigendecompositions, convex analysis, and constrained optimization — the essential mathematical infrastructure for portfolio mathematics.

[Read Appendix A](appendix-a.qmd){.btn-appendix}
:::

::: {.appendix-card}
::: {.label}
Appendix B
:::
### Probability & Statistics for PCRA
Location-scale distributions, least squares (OLS, WLS, GLS), maximum likelihood estimation, asymptotic theory, delta methods, and the Fisher information framework.

[Read Appendix B](appendix-b.qmd){.btn-appendix}
:::

::: {.appendix-card}
::: {.label}
Appendix C
:::
### Utility Theory
Expected utility maximization, concave utility and risk aversion, certainty equivalents, absolute and relative risk aversion, and the relationship between expected utility and mean-variance optimization.

[Read Appendix C](appendix-c.qmd){.btn-appendix}
:::

::: {.appendix-card}
::: {.label}
Appendix D
:::
### Portfolio Theory
Minimum-variance frontier mathematics, the tangent portfolio, CAPM proofs, zero-beta portfolios, APT derivation, and robust estimation details.

[Read Appendix D](appendix-d.qmd){.btn-appendix}
:::

::: {.appendix-card}
::: {.label}
Appendix E
:::
### Machine Learning
Supervised learning, regularization (Ridge, Lasso, Elastic Net), cross-validation, robust regularized regression, logistic regression, and projection pursuit / neural networks.

[Read Appendix E](appendix-e.qmd){.btn-appendix}
:::

:::

---

## How to Use This Site

Each appendix page provides:

- A structured outline of topics covered
- Key figures from the appendix rendered inline
- A downloadable PDF of the full appendix

The appendices are designed to be read alongside the main chapters of PCRA, or independently as reference material. Cross-references to specific sections of the main text appear throughout.

## Citation

If you use material from these appendices in your own work, please cite the book:

> Martin, R. D., Philips, T. K., Scherer, B., and Li, K. (2026). *Portfolio Construction and Risk Analysis*. Springer. (In press.)
